The clip shows a basketball game between Turkey (in red) and Russia (in white). At approximately 0.6–0.8 seconds into the clip, a Turkish player attempts a jump shot near the free-throw line extended. The ball hits the rim and bounces out toward the baseline. Immediately afterward, multiple Russian players converge on the loose ball under the basket. One Russian player secures possession of the ball while being contested by a Turkish defender.

This is a defensive rebound because the shooting team (Turkey) just missed their field goal attempt. In basketball, when a shot fails to enter the basket, the opposing team’s objective is to secure possession via a defensive rebound to prevent the offensive team from getting another scoring opportunity and to initiate their own offense.

The reason the right team (Russia) performs a defensive rebound here is straightforward: they were defending against a missed shot by the opposing team. By grabbing the rebound, Russia disrupted Turkey’s possession, recovered the ball, and created a new offensive opportunity for themselves. This is standard defensive behavior following a missed shot.

Verdict: Yes, the right team performed a defensive rebound because the left team missed a shot, and securing the ball prevents further scoring chances and transitions offense.
